insight.title: Why could you switch tabs and send a stamp?
insight.subtitle: Badges and icons guide your actions
insight.badgeDesc: A red number badge means 'unread messages'. This is an international symbol shared across SNS, email, and messaging apps that naturally guides users.
insight.stampDesc: The 😊 icon means 'open emoji/stamp panel' — a globally universal UI. Even without reading language, this icon's meaning is understood worldwide.
insight.tabDesc: Bottom navigation tabs list 'what you can do in this app'. Icon + number badge naturally guides you to your destination.
insight.inputTitle: Input field is a universal UI
insight.inputDesc: The text input box + send button combination is adopted in chat apps worldwide including WhatsApp, LINE, and Slack. Once experienced, you can intuitively operate it regardless of language.
insight.sendTitle: The → button is a symbol for 'send'
insight.sendDesc: Right-pointing arrow (→) and paper plane icons have become established across cultures as symbols meaning 'send' or 'proceed'. Icons themselves serve as language.
insight.bubbleDesc: Right = me, Left = other — this bubble layout is the de facto standard of chat UI. You can instantly judge who said what using only color and position.

quiz2.missionText: Please react to the other person's message with ❤️

insight.title: How did you find the reaction button?
insight.subtitle: The small icon beside the bubble teaches the action
insight.reactionDesc: Reaction emoji like ❤️ and 👍 are established worldwide across SNS and chat apps as a way to convey emotion without a reply.
insight.iconTitle: The emoji icon beside the speech bubble
insight.iconDesc: The emoji icon shown next to received messages indicates 'you can react to this message'. Even small, its position and shape carry meaning.
insight.heartDesc: Across Instagram, Twitter, LINE, and Slack, the heart means 'positive reaction' — the most widely used emoji symbol regardless of platform.
insight.emojiTitle: Emoji transcend language as emotional expression
insight.emojiDesc: Emoji like 😊 and 👍 convey emotions directly without relying on language. Born in Japan in 1999, this culture is now a global standard.
insight.colorDesc: LINE's green color is deeply ingrained in Japanese people as the symbol of messaging apps. Recognizing by color alone that 'this is a chat app' is the result of repeated UI patterns giving meaning to colors.

insight.title: How did you know the + button could send images?
insight.subtitle: Media sending is handled by the 'add' icon
insight.plusDesc: The + icon on the left of the input field shows 'you can send more than text'. Camera, files, location — various media entry points are hidden here, a universal UI pattern.
insight.photoTitle: 📷 is the universal icon for camera/image
insight.photoDesc: The camera icon doesn't depend on language. It's established in all cultures as a symbol for 'photo/image', and you can predict that tapping it opens image selection.
insight.galleryDesc: The bottom sheet sliding up from below shows 'candidates you can select with this action'. When an image grid appears, selecting and sending becomes intuitively clear.
insight.longPressTitle: Long press is a sign of 'more things you can do'
insight.longPressDesc: UIs that don't respond to a tap often show additional menus on long press. Established as a standard iOS/Android interaction pattern, users unconsciously try it.
insight.contextMenuDesc: The menu appearing on long press lists 'what you can do with this item now'. Patterns with delete, copy, reply options are standard spec in messaging apps.
insight.destructiveDesc: Delete buttons are red to warn about irreversible actions with color. Red = danger/warning is a cultural symbol ingrained from traffic lights, intentionally incorporated into UI design.

insight.title: How did you know long press could unsend?
insight.subtitle: Multiple layers of UI literacy combine to solve it
insight.listDesc: The chat list is ordered by 'most recently messaged'. The top being the most recent talk room is a rule shared across LINE, WhatsApp, iMessage and all messaging apps.
insight.longPressDesc: UIs that don't respond to a tap often show additional menus on long press. Established as a standard iOS/Android interaction, users unconsciously try it.
insight.redDesc: Irreversible actions like unsend and delete are displayed in red by UI design convention. Red = danger, ingrained from traffic lights as a cultural symbol that conveys meaning without reading.
insight.plusTitle: + button is the symbol for 'create new'
insight.plusDesc: The + (plus) icon is a universal symbol meaning 'add' or 'create'. Placed at the bottom right as a FAB (Floating Action Button) is also Material Design standard, intuitively suggesting 'something can be created here'.
insight.wizardTitle: Step format breaks down complex operations
insight.wizardDesc: The flow of group name input → member selection → creation confirmation is a wizard UI pattern that breaks complex actions into small steps. Each step teaches 'what to do next', allowing operation even without understanding language.
insight.checkboxDesc: Checkboxes with ✓ marks intuitively indicate selection state. Used worldwide in forms, member selection, and filters as multi-select UI, their meaning is understood across languages.
